headline: The DHS Shutdown Hits Day 73. Nightmare Airport Lines Could Return Soon.
summary: Travelers could again face longer lines at airport security checkpoints next month if Congress doesn’t figure out a way to fund the Department of Homeland Security this week.  The federal government is on track to run out of emergency funds used to pay some DHS workers during the past month of the department’s 73-day shutdown, including Transportation Security Administration employees that staff airport screening lines.  President Donald Trump had allocated $10 billion in emergency funds for DHS employees in executive orders in March and April.

headline: UPS Q1 Earnings & Revenues Surpass Estimates, Down Year Over Year
summary: United Parcel Service tops Q1 earnings and revenue estimates despite year-over-year declines, and reaffirms 2026 sales guidance above expectations.

headline: Travelers are starting to balk at sky-high airline ticket prices, BofA data shows
summary: Consumers are starting to push back against the airlines.

headline: How JOBY Stock Rises 8x To $70
summary: Joby Aviation’s (JOBY) most critical metric is the "certification timeline" that CEO JoeBen Bevirt uses to anchor investor expectations. While it is often obscured by quarterly cash burn figures, this timeline is the primary driver of Joby’s valuation. This analysis focuses on that specific metric.
